title: Gap design using discovery signal measurements
abstract: A system (85) and method (130, 140, 155) of creating a gap in a network node's angular power profile to reduce inter-cell interference generated at critical directions. A plurality of network nodes (107-109) may jointly derive the gap in the angular power profile of each other. A transmitting node (107) may rely on Discovery Reference 5 Signal, DRS, based Reference Signal Received Power, RSRP, measurements of its beams from a neighboring node (108, 109), which signals an interference metric for each beam indicating a relative beam interference level. The transmitting node utilizes the signaled metrics to determine which beams are causing undesirable interference and are to be removed from its set of candidate beams to create a gap in its angular power 10 profile. The remaining candidate beams are used by the transmitting network node for subsequent directional data transmissions. Alternatively, a central node-based (112) beam removal approach may be implemented.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method (130) in a transmitting network node (107) for reducing inter-cell interference in a cellular telecommunication network (105) in which the transmitting network node utilizes beamforming for directional signal transmission to wireless devices (87-91) operating within a served cell, wherein the transmitting network node dynamically selects a beam from a plurality of candidate beams for transmitting data to one or more of the wireless devices, the method comprising: obtaining (132) a level of inter-cell interference caused in a neighboring cell by transmissions in each of the plurality of candidate beams; removing (137) from the plurality of candidate beams, any beams that caused the level of inter-cell interference in the neighboring cell to exceed a high interference threshold, thereby creating a set of remaining candidate beams; and transmitting (138) data to the wireless devices operating within the served cell utilizing only beams selected from the set of remaining candidate beams. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein obtaining a level of inter-cell interference includes: transmitting a beam-specific reference signal in each of the plurality of candidate beams utilizing a different reference signal resource for each beam; and obtaining a plurality of beam-specific interference metrics corresponding to interference levels in the neighboring cell caused by each of the plurality of candidate beams; wherein a central control node instructs a neighboring network node serving the neighboring cell to request signal quality measurements from wireless devices operating in the neighboring cell, and to calculate and report the beam-specific interference metrics. 3. The method according to claim 1, wherein obtaining a level of inter-cell interference includes: transmitting a beam-specific reference signal in each of the plurality of candidate beams utilizing a different reference signal resource for each beam; instructing a neighboring network node serving the neighboring cell to request 

 from wireless devices operating in the neighboring cell, signal quality measurements of a serving signal and the beam-specific reference signals; and receiving from the neighboring network node, a plurality of beam-specific interference metrics corresponding to the plurality of candidate beams. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein transmitting the beam-specific reference signal includes: using (134) a Discovery Reference Signal (DRS) mechanism to transmit the beam-specific reference signal. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the reference signal resource is a Channel State Information-Reference Signal (CSI-RS) resource. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein instructing the neighboring network node includes: instructing (135) the neighboring network node to Radio Resource Control (RRC) configure the wireless devices operating in the neighboring cell to measure and report a respective Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) value of each beam- specific CSI-RS resource. 7. The method according to claim 5, further comprising performing the method with each of a plurality of neighboring network nodes. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein removing the beams includes: for a beam-specific CSI-RS resource, generating a resource-specific average value of all beam-specific interference metrics received from the plurality of neighboring network nodes for a candidate beam mapped to the beam-specific CSI-RS resource; for the beam-specific CSI-RS resource, comparing the resource-specific average value against the high interference threshold; and removing the candidate beam mapped to the beam-specific CSI-RS resource from the plurality of candidate beams if the resource-specific average value exceeds the 

 high interference threshold. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein removing the beams includes one of the following: for each candidate beam in the plurality of candidate beams, generating a beam- specific average value of all corresponding beam-specific interference metrics received from the plurality of neighboring network nodes and removing the candidate beam from the plurality of candidate beams if the beam-specific average value exceeds the high interference threshold; and for each candidate beam in the plurality of candidate beams, removing the candidate beam from the plurality of candidate beams based on the beam-specific average value as well as relative usage of the candidate beam by wireless devices served by the transmitting network node. 10. The method of claim 7, wherein removing the beams includes: selecting a group of beams from the plurality of candidate beams, wherein each beam in the group is selected based on all corresponding beam-specific interference metrics received from the plurality of neighboring network nodes; creating a subset of beams from the group of beams; for each beam in the subset, calculating a beam-specific Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) loss metric for each wireless device that is currently served by the beam in the subset, wherein the beam-specific RSRP loss metric for a wireless device represents a difference between a first RSRP of the beam in the subset that is currently serving the wireless device and a second RSRP of a best-available beam from the plurality of candidate beams that the wireless device is able to choose if the subset of beams is removed from the plurality of candidate beams; and removing each beam in the subset from the plurality of candidate beams based on a comparison of each beam-specific RSRP loss metric with an average of all beam- specific interference metrics received from the plurality of neighboring network nodes. 11. The method of claim 3, wherein a beam-specific interference metric in the plurality of beam-specific interference metrics is a function of the following: 

 a first Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) value of the serving signal; and a second RSRP value of a beam-specific Channel State Information-Reference Signal (CSI-RS) resource measured by a wireless device operating in the neighboring cell. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the function is a difference between the first and the second RSRP values. 13. The method of claim 3, wherein each of the transmitting network node and the neighboring network node is one of the following: a Radio Base Station (RBS); an evolved Node B (eNodeB); and a node used as a base station. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein the transmitting node utilizes elevation beamforming, and wherein each candidate beam is a candidate elevation beam. 15. A method (140) of reporting of inter-cell interference information by a first network node (108, 109) to a second network node (107) associated with the first network node, wherein the second network node utilizes beamforming for directional signal transmission to wireless devices (87-91) operating within a served cell, and wherein the method comprises performing the following by the first network node: receiving (142) an instruction from the second network node; in response to the instruction, obtaining (146), from one or more wireless devices served by the first network node, a device -specific Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) value of a beam-specific reference signal transmitted by the second network node in a beam; generating (150) a beam-specific interference metric for the beam based on all reported device-specific RSRP values, wherein the beam-specific interference metric represents a level of inter-cell interference caused by the beam in a neighboring cell served by the first network node; and 

 transmitting (152) the beam-specific interference metric to the second network node. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein receiving the instruction includes: receiving (144) the instruction as part of a Discovery Reference Signal (DRS) procedure. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein obtaining the device-specific RSRP value includes: using (148) Radio Resource Control (RRC) protocol to obtain the device- specific RSRP value of a beam-specific Channel State Information-Reference Signal (CSI-RS) resource transmitted by the second network node for the beam. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein generating the beam-specific interference metric includes: for each wireless device served by the first network node, generating a device- specific interference metric as a function of the following: the received device-specific RSRP value of the beam-specific CSI-RS resource, and an RSRP value of a serving beam used by the first network node for the wireless device; and combining device-specific interference metrics of two or more wireless devices served by the first network node to generate the beam-specific interference metric. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the function is a difference between the received device-specific RSRP value and the RSRP value of the serving beam. 20. The method of claim 18, wherein combining the device-specific interference metrics includes: obtaining a weighted average of the device-specific interference metrics of the two or more wireless devices to generate the beam-specific interference metric. 21. The method of claim 18, wherein the method comprises further performing the following by the first network node: for a target wireless device served by the first network node, determining that the device -specific interference metric of the target wireless device is above a pre- defined threshold; and issuing a handover command to handover the target wireless device to the second network node. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the first network node employs beamforming and utilizes the serving beam for directional signal transmission to the target wireless device, and wherein the method comprises further performing the following by the first network node: removing the serving beam from a set of beams used by the first network node for data transmissions. 23. The method of claim 15, wherein transmitting the beam-specific interference metric includes one of the following: directly signaling entire value of the beam-specific interference metric to the second network node; signaling the beam-specific interference metric to the second network node only if the beam-specific interference metric is above a pre-defined threshold; and using a single bit to indirectly signal the value of the beam-specific interference metric to the second network node. 24. The method of claim 15, wherein each of the first and the second network nodes utilizes elevation beamforming, and wherein the beam transmitted by the second network node is an elevation beam. 25. A method (155) of managing inter-cell interference in a cellular network (105) in which a transmitting network node (107) utilizes beamforming for directional signal transmission to wireless devices (87-91) operating within a served cell, wherein the transmitting network node dynamically selects a beam from a plurality of candidate 

 beams for transmitting data to one or more of the wireless devices and is in operative communication with a central node (112), wherein the method comprises performing the following by the central node: obtaining (157) a level of inter-cell interference caused in a neighboring cell by transmissions in each of the plurality of candidate beams; identifying (159) any beams that caused the level of inter-cell interference in the neighboring cell to exceed a high interference threshold; performing (161) one of the following based on the identification: removing any identified beams from the plurality of candidate beams, thereby creating a set of remaining candidate beams, or instructing the transmitting network node to remove any identified beams from the plurality of candidate beams and to thereby create the set of remaining candidate beams; and instructing (163) the transmitting network node to transmit data to the wireless devices operating within the served cell utilizing only beams selected from the set of remaining candidate beams. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein obtaining the level of inter-cell interference includes: instructing the transmitting network node to transmit a beam-specific reference signal in each of the plurality of candidate beams; instructing a neighboring network node serving the neighboring cell to provide a beam-specific interference metric for each beam in the plurality of candidate beams, wherein each beam-specific interference metric represents a level of interference posed by a corresponding beam in the plurality of candidate beams at one or more User Equipments (UEs) served by the neighboring network node; and receiving a plurality of beam interference metrics from the neighboring network node; and wherein identifying the beams includes: using the received plurality of beam interference metrics to determine which one or more beams cause the level of inter-cell interference in the neighboring cell to exceed a high interference threshold. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein instructing the neighboring network node includes: instructing the neighboring network node, using a Discovery Reference Signal (DRS) procedure, to Radio Resource Control (RRC) configure one or more UEs served thereby to measure and report a respective UE-specific Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) value of each beam-specific reference signal transmitted by the transmitting network node; and further instructing the neighboring network node to generate the beam-specific interference metric for each beam in the plurality of candidate beams based on all reported UE-specific RSRP values. 28. The method of claim 25, wherein the transmitting node utilizes elevation beamforming, and wherein each candidate beam is a candidate elevation beam. 29. A transmitting network node (172) in a cellular network for performing data transmissions using beamforming, wherein the transmitting network node comprises: a transceiver (177) operable to perform the following: transmit a beam-specific reference signal in each beam in a first set of beams, and receive a node-specific set of beam interference metrics from each receiving node in a plurality of receiving nodes associated with the transmitting network node; a memory (182, 185) for storing program instructions; and a processor (181) coupled to the transceiver and the memory and operable to execute the program instructions, which, when executed by the processor, cause the transmitting network node to perform the following: generate the beam-specific reference signal for each beam in the first set of beams, instruct each receiving node to provide a beam-specific interference metric for each beam in the first set of beams, wherein each beam-specific interference metric represents a level of interference caused by a corresponding beam in the first set 

 of beams in a neighboring cell served by a respective receiving node, use received sets of beam interference metrics to determine which one or more beams are to be removed from the first set of beams to obtain a second set of beams, wherein the second set of beams is a subset of the first set of beams, and perform data transmissions using the transceiver and utilizing only the beams from the second set of beams. 30. The transmitting network node of claim 29, wherein the transmitting network node is one of the following: a Radio Base Station (RBS); an evolved Node B (eNodeB); and a node used as a base station. 31. The transmitting network node of claim 29, wherein the program instructions, when executed by the processor, cause the transmitting network node to further perform the following: map each beam in the first set of beams to a beam-specific Channel State Information-Reference Signal (CSI-RS) resource; using the transceiver, transmit the beam-specific reference signal in each beam in the first set of beams using a corresponding beam-specific CSI-RS resource; instruct each receiving node to Radio Resource Control (RRC) configure wireless devices attached thereto to provide a respective Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) value of each beam-specific CSI-RS resource transmitted by the transmitting network node; for a beam-specific CSI-RS resource, generate a resource-specific average value of all beam-specific interference metrics received from the plurality of receiving nodes for a beam mapped to the beam-specific CSI-RS resource; for the beam-specific CSI-RS resource, compare the resource-specific average value against a pre-defined threshold; and remove the beam mapped to the beam-specific CSI-RS resource from the first set if the resource-specific average value exceeds the pre-defined threshold. 32. The transmitting network node of claim 29, wherein the transmitting node utilizes elevation beamforming, and wherein each beam in the first set of beams is an elevation beam.
